result.summary: HubSpot exposes authenticated HTTP APIs at `https://api.hubapi.com` (general APIs via OAuth bearer tokens, static auth bearer tokens, and some app-management endpoints via `hapikey` + `appId`), a separate authenticated Webhooks Journal/Management API using client-credentials bearer tokens, and an authenticated `hs` CLI that logs in with a personal access key.

oauth_app.label: HubSpot OAuth app client credentials
oauth_app.setup: Create an app in your HubSpot developer account, then open the app in [Manage apps in HubSpot](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/manage-apps-in-hubspot). On the app’s **Auth** tab, copy the `Client ID` and `Client secret`, and configure your `redirectUrls` in `app-hsmeta.json` / the app settings. Use these credentials to run the OAuth install flow described in [Working with OAuth](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/oauth/working-with-oauth).

static_auth_token.label: HubSpot static auth access token
static_auth_token.setup: Create a private app on HubSpot’s developer platform, open it in [Manage apps in HubSpot](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/manage-apps-in-hubspot), and use the install flow for a static token app. The token is available from the app settings/Auth area as described in [Authentication overview](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/overview). This token only installs to a single authorized account at a time.

developer_api_key.label: HubSpot developer API key
developer_api_key.setup: Open your HubSpot developer account’s app/developer overview and go to **Keys** / developer key management as described in [Manage apps in HubSpot](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/manage-apps-in-hubspot) and [Authentication overview](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/overview#developer-api-keys). Copy the developer API key and, for endpoints that require it, send it as the `hapikey` query parameter, usually together with the app’s `appId`.

app_id.label: HubSpot app ID
app_id.setup: Create or open your app in [Manage apps in HubSpot](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/manage-apps-in-hubspot). Use the app’s identifier (`appId`) alongside a developer API key for certain app-management endpoints, as noted in [Authentication overview](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/overview#developer-api-keys).

client_credentials_token.label: HubSpot client-credentials token for webhook journal/management APIs
client_credentials_token.setup: Create or open your HubSpot app and copy its `Client ID` and `Client secret` from the app’s **Auth** tab in [Manage apps in HubSpot](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/manage-apps-in-hubspot). Then request a token from `https://api.hubapi.com/oauth/2026-03/token` with `grant_type=client_credentials` and the required `developer.webhooks_journal.*` scopes as shown in [Authentication overview](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/overview#client-credentials) and the [Webhooks journal guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/api-reference/latest/webhooks-journal/guide).

personal_access_key.label: HubSpot personal access key for CLI/local tooling
personal_access_key.setup: Install the HubSpot CLI, run `hs account auth`, and follow the prompts to generate or reveal your personal access key, as described in [Install the HubSpot CLI](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/developer-tooling/local-development/hubspot-cli/install-the-cli) and [Authenticate the CLI with personal access keys](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/developer-tooling/local-development/hubspot-cli/personal-access-key). You can also open the key page directly at [Personal Access Key](http://app.hubspot.com/l/personal-access-key). Paste the key into the CLI when prompted; it is stored in `~/.hscli/config.yml`.
